Wheat Head: [{'Wheat Head': "The reproductive structure of cereal plants such as wheat or barley, consisting of a dense, elongated cluster of spikelets arranged along a central rachis. Each spikelet contains florets that develop into grains, forming a compact, cylindrical or slightly tapered head. The structure is rigid and organized, with a bristly texture and a distinct separation from the surrounding leaves, which are typically long, flat, and linear. It is attached to the plant at the base of the stem and remains structurally intact during development, serving as the primary site of seed production. This head is distinguishable from fragmented or dry plant material by its cohesive, well-defined morphology and functional role in the plant's life cycle."}]
